names.japanese_name: 酸化ポリエチレンワックス
names.japanese_label_name: 酸化ポリエチレンワックス

natural_occurrence.description: A synthetic wax produced by controlled oxidation of low molecular weight polyethylene. Used as a glazing agent and release agent in confectionery. Chemically similar to plastic but food-grade and highly purified.

manufacturing.description: Produced by polymerization of ethylene to form low molecular weight polyethylene, followed by controlled oxidation to introduce polar functional groups (carboxylic acids, esters). Highly purified for food-grade applications.

other_uses.industrial: Printing inks, hot-melt adhesives, PVC processing, rubber
other_uses.household: Floor polish, car wax, textile treatment

eu.conditions: Authorized as glazing agent for surface treatment; only food-grade oxidized PE wax permitted

japan.japanese_regulation_name: 酸化ポリエチレンワックス

canada.reference: Lists of Permitted Glazing and Polishing Agents

safety_evaluations.efsa_adi: Not specified (no safety concern at current use levels)

regulatory_history.event: JECFA and EFSA evaluate oxidized polyethylene wax for food use

adi_context.source_note: No ADI established - oxidized polyethylene wax is not absorbed by the digestive system and passes through unchanged.

fun_facts: Chemically related to plastic bags - but food-grade and highly purified
Made from the same ethylene gas used to ripen fruit
Passes through your body completely unchanged
Used in both candy coating and car wax
Oxidation makes it more polar and better at coating than regular polyethylene
One of several synthetic waxes that replaced natural waxes in food
